name: multi-tab-batch
description: Verify batchAction runs the same action on multiple tabs in parallel and returns per-tab results.

steps.expect: async (response, ctx) => { const v = ctx.agent.deps.getActiveView(); const r = await ctx.agent.batchAction([1, 1, 1], 'getVisibleText'); return [ {ok: r.ok === true, message: 'batchAction should succeed'}, {ok: r.action === 'getVisibleText', message: 'action echoed back'}, {ok: r.tabCount === 3, message: 'should report tabCount'}, {ok: r.results.length === 3, message: 'should have 3 results'}, {ok: r.results.every(x => x.ok === true), message: 'all 3 results should be ok'}, {ok: r.results.every(x => typeof x.tabId === 'number'), message: 'each result has tabId'} ]; }

async (response, ctx) => { const r = await ctx.agent.batchAction([1], 'click'); return [ {ok: r.ok === false, message: 'non-allowed action should fail'}, {ok: r.error.includes('not batchable'), message: 'should explain why'} ]; }
steps.note: Rejects write actions (click) — only read-only batchable

steps.expect: async (response, ctx) => { const ids = Array.from({length: 25}, (_, i) => i + 1); const r = await ctx.agent.batchAction(ids, 'inspect'); return [ {ok: r.ok === false, message: 'too many tabs should fail'}, {ok: r.error.includes('too many'), message: 'should explain limit'} ]; }
steps.note: Enforces max 20 tabs per batch (hangs prevention)

steps.expect: async (response, ctx) => { const r = await ctx.agent.batchAction([], 'inspect'); return [ {ok: r.ok === false, message: 'empty tabIds should fail'} ]; }
